ops_incidents.title: API gateway returning 502 errors for 30% of requests
ops_incidents.description: Starting at 14:32 UTC, approximately 30% of API requests through the gateway are returning 502 Bad Gateway errors. Affected endpoints include /api/v1/users and /api/v1/data. Customer-facing dashboards showing intermittent failures.
ops_incidents.resolution: Root cause was an exhausted connection pool in the API service. Increased max connections from 100 to 500 and added connection pool monitoring. Service recovered at 15:18 UTC.
ops_incidents.root_cause: Connection pool exhaustion due to slow database queries during peak traffic. No circuit breaker was configured for the downstream database connection.

ops_incidents.title: Database replication lag exceeding 30 seconds
ops_incidents.description: Read replica lag spiked to 30+ seconds during batch processing window. Read-heavy endpoints serving stale data. Autoscaling triggered additional read replicas but lag persisted.
ops_incidents.resolution: Identified a long-running analytical query locking rows on the primary. Killed the query, optimized it with proper indexing, and rescheduled to off-peak hours.
ops_incidents.root_cause: Unoptimized analytical query running during peak hours caused write amplification on the primary, overwhelming replication throughput.

ops_incidents.title: Redis cluster node failure causing cache misses
ops_incidents.description: One of three Redis cluster nodes went unresponsive. Cluster auto-failover promoted a replica but 15% of keys were unavailable during the 90-second failover window. Elevated latency on all cache-dependent services.
ops_incidents.resolution: Cluster auto-failover completed successfully. Replaced the failed node hardware. Added memory monitoring alerts at 80% threshold (previous alert was only at 95%).
ops_incidents.root_cause: Out-of-memory condition on the Redis node due to a memory leak in a Lua script used for rate limiting. Script was accumulating state without TTL.

ops_incidents.title: SSL certificate expiration on webhook endpoint
ops_incidents.description: TLS certificate for webhooks.platform.com expired at 00:00 UTC. All outbound webhook deliveries failing with SSL errors. Certificate auto-renewal failed silently 3 days ago.
ops_incidents.resolution: Manually renewed the certificate via cert-manager. Fixed the auto-renewal config (DNS-01 challenge was pointed at wrong zone). Added certificate expiration monitoring with 30-day and 7-day alerts.
ops_incidents.root_cause: DNS zone migration broke the cert-manager DNS-01 challenge configuration. Auto-renewal failure was not alerting because the monitoring only checked the live cert, not the renewal job.

ops_incidents.title: Elevated error rate on /search endpoint after deploy
ops_incidents.description: Following deploy v2.14.0 at 16:00 UTC, the /search endpoint error rate increased from 0.1% to 8.2%. Errors are 500 Internal Server Error with null pointer exceptions in the search indexer.
ops_incidents.resolution: Rolled back to v2.13.9. Root cause was a missing null check on a new optional field in the search index mapping. Fixed in v2.14.1 with proper null handling and additional test coverage.
ops_incidents.root_cause: Code change in v2.14.0 introduced a new search field without null safety. Integration tests did not cover the case of existing records lacking the new field.

ops_incidents.title: Scheduled maintenance: Database version upgrade
ops_incidents.description: Planned maintenance window for PostgreSQL upgrade from 15.4 to 16.1. Expected 10-minute downtime window during primary switchover. All services will failover to read-only mode during the window.
ops_incidents.resolution: Upgrade completed successfully in 7 minutes. All services resumed normal operation. Post-upgrade query performance improved 12% on average due to query planner improvements in PG16.

ops_deployments.change_summary: Fix null pointer in search indexer, add null safety for optional fields, increase test coverage for search mapping
Add full-text search indexing for new content fields, update search API response format
Add retry with exponential backoff for failed webhook deliveries, improve connection pooling
New real-time metrics aggregation pipeline, optimize query performance for time-series data
Major version: migrate from JWT to opaque tokens, add token introspection endpoint, improve session management
Fix email template rendering for non-ASCII characters, add Slack webhook retry logic
Add Snowflake sink connector, improve Kafka consumer offset management, reduce memory footprint by 30%

ops_services.name: core-api
ops_services.description: Primary REST API serving all frontend and integration requests. Handles authentication, workspace management, pipeline configuration, and billing.

ops_services.name: pipeline-worker
ops_services.description: Unified binary handling both CDC ingestion and sink delivery. Processes events from per-environment Redis queues via Asynq.

ops_services.name: webhook-service
ops_services.description: Handles outbound webhook delivery with retry logic, signature verification, and delivery tracking.

ops_services.name: analytics-service
ops_services.description: Aggregates and serves metrics dashboards including pipeline throughput, event counts, and error rates.

ops_services.name: auth-service
ops_services.description: Handles user authentication, authorization, session management, and SSO integrations (SAML, OIDC).

ops_services.name: notification-service
ops_services.description: Manages email, Slack, and in-app notifications for alerts, reports, and system events.

ops_runbooks.title: Core API: High Error Rate Response
ops_runbooks.steps:
1. Check Grafana dashboard for error rate trend and affected endpoints
2. Check pod logs: kubectl logs -l app=core-api --tail=100
3. Verify database connectivity: run health check query
4. Check connection pool metrics in /debug/pprof
5. If connection pool exhausted: scale horizontally or increase pool size
6. If database issue: check replica lag, failover if needed
7. If application bug: rollback to last known good version
8. Update status page and notify affected customers
ops_runbooks.author: Alice Chen

ops_runbooks.title: Pipeline Worker: KEDA Scaling Troubleshooting
ops_runbooks.steps:
1. Check KEDA ScaledObject status: kubectl get scaledobject -n {namespace}
2. Verify Asynq queue depth: check Redis key asynq:{queue}:pending
3. Check KEDA operator logs for scaling decisions
4. Verify Redis connectivity from KEDA trigger auth
5. If not scaling: check trigger auth secret, verify queue name matches
6. If scaling but pods failing: check resource limits, node capacity
7. Manual scale override: kubectl scale deployment pipeline-worker --replicas=N
ops_runbooks.author: David Park

ops_runbooks.title: Database: PostgreSQL Failover Procedure
ops_runbooks.steps:
1. Confirm primary is unresponsive (check pg_isready, attempt connection)
2. Verify replica lag is <5 seconds before promoting
3. Promote replica: pg_ctl promote -D /var/lib/postgresql/data
4. Update DNS/service endpoint to point to new primary
5. Verify all application connections are using new primary
6. Start new replica from backup of promoted primary
7. Verify replication streaming is established
8. Run data integrity checks on key tables
9. Update runbook with any procedure changes
ops_runbooks.author: Marcus Rivera

ops_runbooks.title: SSL Certificate Renewal
ops_runbooks.steps:
1. Check certificate expiration: echo | openssl s_client -connect host:443 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates
2. If cert-manager auto-renewal failed: check cert-manager logs
3. Verify DNS challenge configuration matches current DNS zones
4. Force renewal: kubectl delete certificate {name} -n {namespace}
5. Monitor cert-manager for new certificate issuance
6. Verify new certificate is served: curl -vI https://host
7. Update monitoring for certificate expiration alerts
ops_runbooks.author: Emily Torres

ops_runbooks.title: Redis Cluster: Node Recovery
ops_runbooks.steps:
1. Identify failed node: redis-cli --cluster check {cluster-ip}:6379
2. Verify cluster is still serving requests (check remaining nodes)
3. If replica promoted automatically: verify new topology
4. Replace failed node: provision new instance with same config
5. Add new node to cluster: redis-cli --cluster add-node
6. Rebalance slots if needed: redis-cli --cluster rebalance
7. Verify cluster health: redis-cli --cluster info
8. Check for memory leaks in Lua scripts: MEMORY DOCTOR
ops_runbooks.author: Marcus Rivera

ops_runbooks.title: Production Deployment Rollback
ops_runbooks.steps:
1. Confirm the deployment issue (error rate spike, health check failures)
2. Check current version: kubectl get deployment {service} -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}'
3. Identify rollback target version from deployment history
4. Execute rollback: kubectl rollout undo deployment/{service}
5. Monitor rollout: kubectl rollout status deployment/{service}
6. Verify error rate returns to baseline
7. Create incident ticket for the failed deployment
8. Block the broken version from re-deployment
ops_runbooks.author: Alice Chen
